question: Looking at the unemployment rate map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: The unemployment rate gap between Idaho and Louisiana might exceed that between Michigan and Montana.
options.B: The highlighted states have very diverse unemployment rate levels overall.
options.C: The unemployment rate differences are impossible to determine from this visualization.
options.D: Idaho and Louisiana have similar unemployment rates.

explanation: Idaho (11.8%) and Louisiana (14.5%) have a 2.7% unemployment rate difference, while Michigan (5.5%) and Montana (6.3%) have only a 0.8% difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Idaho and Louisiana into the same color bin (10%+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Michigan and Montana appear more different due to different color bins.
